sprint.name: Forge Expansion Sprint
sprint.note: A scheduled review is not a guaranteed release. The forge publishes at most one coherent change when it clears the quality and safety gates; otherwise it skips the window.
sprint.secondaryAutomation: A separate GitHub Actions workflow runs Tuesday and Friday at 15:17 UTC. It can only create or improve approved declarative-engine apps and records accepted publishes in this ledger.

method.label: Inspect
method.description: Review the live site, repository, current apps, open feedback, bugs, depth, accessibility, and portfolio gaps.

method.label: Compare
method.description: Consider at least three candidate changes and choose the one that creates the strongest useful depth or breadth.

method.label: Build one
method.description: Create or improve one coherent feature or app, including mobile, keyboard, touch, reduced-motion, teardown, and edge cases.

method.label: Validate
method.description: Run JavaScript syntax checks, feedback-routing tests, registry rules, required-file checks, and safety-boundary validation.

method.label: Publish or skip
method.description: Merge and deploy only after every required check passes. Skip when the candidate is weak, unsafe, duplicative, or incomplete.

qualityGates: One coherent change maximum per review window
No secrets, trackers, accounts, hidden storage, or unreviewed network behavior
No direct execution of user feedback
Mobile, keyboard, touch, focus, and reduced-motion behavior considered
Repository validation must pass before main is published

queue.title: Ambient Ambient adaptive room masker
queue.reason: Potentially valuable audio experiment, but MediaDevices permission, microphone privacy, and audio teardown require a dedicated security review before implementation.

queue.title: Interpreter Dispatch language-access balance playtest
queue.reason: Collect evidence on request urgency clarity, language-versus-specialty tradeoffs, remote-quality balance, fatigue pacing, mobile list density, keyboard selection, mutual-aid recovery, and whether five shifts create enough strategic variation without exhausting the service pool.

queue.title: Wayfinding Workshop inclusive-route playtest
queue.reason: Collect evidence on procedural route fairness, sign-budget clarity, traveler-profile differentiation, keyboard focus restoration, narrow-screen cell density, tactile-beacon pacing, and whether late accessibility review enables recovery without erasing trust consequences.

queue.title: Pulse Loom arrangement balance playtest
queue.reason: Collect evidence on brief clarity, energy pricing, accent unlock pacing, keyboard grid movement, narrow-screen density, optional audio teardown, and whether halftime recovery supports experimentation without erasing crowd consequences.

queue.title: Blackout Atlas restoration balance playtest
queue.reason: Collect evidence on hidden-fault readability, route-planning depth, cascade fairness, critical-load urgency, mobile grid density, keyboard movement, battery value, and whether mutual aid creates recovery without erasing trust consequences.

queue.title: Common Ground mediation balance playtest
queue.reason: Collect evidence on hidden-need clarity, action-cost comprehension, objection fairness, mobile action density, agreement thresholds, and whether the cooling break enables recovery without erasing sequencing consequences.

queue.title: Succession Plot biome balance playtest
queue.reason: Collect evidence on forecast-corridor clarity, biome difficulty, adjacency bonuses, mobile cell density, keyboard movement, and whether the permanent emergency-recovery penalty remains consequential without making recovery futile.

queue.title: Demand Ripple operations balance playtest
queue.reason: Collect evidence on lead-time comprehension, order-slider discoverability, shock fairness, bullwhip readability, mobile chart legibility, forecast-upgrade value, and whether emergency credit supports recovery without trivializing cash pressure.

queue.title: Anomaly Array spatial deduction playtest
queue.reason: Collect evidence on clue readability, shift fairness, touch target density at 7×7, scan usefulness, keyboard focus movement, and whether prediction rewards deduction rather than lucky marking before adding shapes.

queue.title: Repair Bench diagnostic balance playtest
queue.reason: Collect evidence on confidence readability, two-fault difficulty, keyboard discoverability, narrow-screen evidence scanning, and whether recovery meaningfully supports learning before adding machines.

queue.title: Compost Cascade balance playtest
queue.reason: Collect evidence on order pacing, contamination pressure, double-click discoverability, narrow-screen readability, and whether recovery creates a fair comeback before expanding seasons.

queue.title: Council Relay role-boundary playtest
queue.reason: Collect evidence on pass-device privacy, solo readability, role balance, and whether the three-role loop remains understandable on narrow mobile screens before expanding scenarios.

recentDecisions.title: Interpreter Dispatch 1.0.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared a language-access interpreter dispatch game, a branching public-health triage simulation, and a cooperative emergency mapper. Prioritized Interpreter Dispatch because multilingual matching, specialty fit, remote-versus-onsite tradeoffs, fatigue, time, budget, trust, access quality, procedural requests, a session unlock, failure, and one costly mutual-aid recovery add an underserved language-access operations loop rather than another emergency-routing or generic triage variant.
checks: Language, specialty, urgency and wait accounting
Enhanced remote unlock and one-use recovery
Reduced motion, teardown and safety wiring

recentDecisions.title: Wayfinding Workshop 1.0.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared an inclusive wayfinding design game, a branching public-health triage simulation, and a cooperative emergency mapper. Prioritized Wayfinding Workshop because procedural floor plans, traveler-specific accessibility needs, sign placement, route testing, constrained budgets, trust, scoring, an unlockable tactile beacon, failure, and one costly late-review recovery add an underserved accessibility and spatial-design loop rather than another infrastructure or inventory simulation.
checks: Four sign types and budget accounting
Responsive touch and keyboard grid controls
Reduced motion, teardown and safety wiring

recentDecisions.title: Pulse Loom 1.0.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared a cooperative emergency mapper, a branching public-health triage simulation, and a tactile arrangement game. Prioritized Pulse Loom because a four-lane step sequencer, evolving crowd briefs, resource-priced pattern editing, optional audio, combo scoring, an unlockable accent lane, six-set progression, failure, and one costly halftime recovery add an underserved creative music-system loop rather than another civic, ecology, inventory, or grid-restoration simulation.
checks: Energy, crowd, combo and score accounting
Optional user-initiated Web Audio with teardown
Responsive touch and keyboard grid controls

recentDecisions.title: Blackout Atlas 1.0.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared a procedural blackout restoration planner, a tactile music-arrangement game, and a branching public-health triage simulation. Prioritized Blackout Atlas because hidden line faults, connected power propagation, route planning, scarce crews and parts, critical facilities, batteries, cascading failures, trust pressure, scoring, failure, and one costly mutual-aid recovery add an underserved network-restoration loop rather than another mediation, ecology, or inventory simulation.
checks: Hidden fault inspection and repair accounting
Critical-load, crew, part, battery and trust tradeoffs
Responsive touch and keyboard grid controls
Reduced motion, teardown and safety wiring

recentDecisions.title: Common Ground 1.0.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared a cooperative emergency mapper, a tactile music-arrangement game, and a branching mediation workshop. Prioritized Common Ground because hidden stakeholder needs, evidence gathering, trust and tension, limited time, adaptive objections, commitment drafting, durable-versus-fragile outcomes, failure, and one costly recovery add an underserved narrative social-strategy loop rather than another spatial or resource-routing simulation.
checks: Listening, evidence and clause state transitions
Trust, tension, time and insight accounting
Reduced motion, teardown and safety wiring

recentDecisions.title: Succession Plot 1.1.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared a cooperative emergency mapper, a branching mediation game, and a deeper ecology simulation. Prioritized Succession Plot because three biome modes, visible disturbance corridors, mixed-species protection, shrub and canopy adjacency synergies, biome-specific goals, and consequential recovery directly address readability and strategic-depth gaps without adding another overlapping simulation.
checks: Failure pressure and permanent recovery penalty
Responsive touch and keyboard grid controls
Reduced motion, teardown and safety wiring

recentDecisions.title: Demand Ripple 1.0.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared a supply-chain bullwhip simulation, a cooperative emergency-mapping game, and an ecosystem succession sandbox. Prioritized Demand Ripple because delayed fulfillment, noisy demand, inventory and cash pressure, service tradeoffs, policy modes, forecasting upgrades, structural shocks, failure, and emergency recovery add an underserved business-systems loop rather than another spatial or environmental routing game.
checks: Cash, service, backlog and bullwhip scoring
Responsive canvas chart and mobile controls
Keyboard, reduced motion, teardown and safety wiring

recentDecisions.title: Anomaly Array 1.0.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared a language-light spatial deduction game, a cooperative emergency-mapping game, and a narrative branching workshop. Prioritized Anomaly Array because exact row and column clues, connected hidden shapes, scarce scans, suspect marking, moving unresolved signals, streak scoring, failure, and escalating grids add an underserved deduction loop rather than another resource-routing simulation.
checks: Scans, marks, probes, shifts and prediction
Responsive touch and keyboard grid controls

recentDecisions.title: Repair Bench 1.0.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared a diagnostic repair bench, a cooperative emergency-mapping game, and an accessibility-first spatial puzzle. Prioritized Repair Bench because reversible testing, confidence-building, interacting faults, spare-part risk, time pressure, damaging guesses, recovery, and machine unlocks add an underserved maintenance and diagnosis loop rather than another resource-routing simulation.
checks: Time, spare, trust, and damage accounting

recentDecisions.title: Compost Cascade 1.0.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared a circular compost operations game, a cooperative emergency-mapping game, and a tactile repair-sequencing puzzle. Prioritized Compost Cascade because transformation timing, pile chemistry, changing demand, contamination, upgrades, shocks, and recovery add a new circular-systems strategy loop rather than another routing or council variant.
checks: Orders, upgrades, shocks, failure and recovery
